name: The Legions' Footprint - circular trail running from Fuentes de Peñacorada
name_original: La Huella de las Legiones –  trail running circular desde Fuentes de Peñacorada
region_intro: Jogging routes Cistierna offer diverse terrain within the province of León, Spain, characterized by its mountainous landscape and scenic reservoirs. The region features iconic mountains and hills, providing varied ascents and descents for runners. Additionally, the area includes the serene Riaño Reservoir and its "Leonese Fjords," alongside extensive forests and riverine paths. These natural features create a range of running environments, from challenging mountain trails to picturesque waterside routes. Best jogging routes around Cistierna The most popular jogging route is Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6), a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route features rolling hills and passes through the historic mining landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is The Legions' Footprint - circular trail running from Fuentes de Peñacorada, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the surrounding hills and valleys, often completed in under an hour. Local runners also love the Mercadillo Bridge – Puente del Mercadillo loop from Cistierna, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through riverine areas and local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Jogging around Cistierna is defined by mountainous terrain, the tranquil Riaño Reservoir, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult trails. The routes in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cistierna, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Cistierna offers 10 distinct running routes. You'll find a good mix, with 4 routes classified as moderate and 6 as difficult, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes around Cistierna?
A: The jogging routes in Cistierna feature diverse terrain, from mountainous paths with ascents and descents to scenic trails along the tranquil Riaño Reservoir and through extensive forests. You can expect varied surfaces, offering both challenging trail running and more picturesque waterside options.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Cistierna area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Cistierna are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, which is a moderate 8.9 km loop. Another popular option is &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052783646/the-legions-footprint-circular-trail-running-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Legions' Footprint - circular trail running from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a 6.9 km moderate path.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest I can see while jogging in Cistierna?
A: While jogging in Cistierna, you can encounter several scenic points. The region is known for its dramatic 'Leonese Fjords' and the beautiful Riaño Reservoir, offering stunning views. Some routes might pass by highlights such as the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Mercadillo&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; or provide expansive &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. The mountainous terrain also offers panoramic vistas, particularly from higher elevations like those near &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: How long do the running routes in Cistierna typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running routes in Cistierna varies depending on their length and difficulty. For instance, the moderate &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; typically takes about 1 hour 5 minutes, while the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31910693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercadillo Bridge – Puente del Mercadillo loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; usually takes around 1 hour 12 minutes. More challenging or longer routes, like &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Espinadal – View of Fuentes de Peñacorada loop&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, can take over 1 hour 50 minutes.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Cistierna?
A: The running routes in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from historic mining areas to scenic riverine paths and challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Cistierna?
A: While Cistierna is known for its moderate to difficult routes, some sections of trails along the Riaño Reservoir or within the riverine areas might offer flatter, more accessible terrain suitable for beginners. It's recommended to check the elevation profile of specific routes on komoot to find the most suitable options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Cistierna?
A: Cistierna's natural environment, including its forests and riverine areas, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, it's always best to check local regulations or specific route details for any restrictions, especially within protected areas like the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park, and to keep your dog under control.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Cistierna?
A: The diverse landscape of Cistierna makes it suitable for jogging across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for exploring both mountain and waterside trails. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter jogging is possible, but higher mountain trails may be affected by snow and ice, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Riaño Reservoir?
A: Yes, the shores of the Riaño Reservoir are highlighted as one of the most beautiful spots in the region, perfect for picturesque jogging paths. While specific routes are not detailed in the guide, the general area around the reservoir provides stunning views of the 'Leonese Fjords' and is ideal for enjoying nature while running.

Q: Where can I find parking near the trailheads in Cistierna?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting from specific villages, such as the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas from Sabero&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; or &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052783646/the-legions-footprint-circular-trail-running-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Legions' Footprint from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, you can often find parking within or near those villages. It's advisable to check the starting points on komoot for specific parking information or local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through historic areas?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is a prime example. This moderate route passes through a historic mining landscape, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and industrial heritage as you run.
